name: Casareccio (Rustic Wholewheat Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Pitted Italian black olives - optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the yeast into the warm water and let proof for 10 minutes.
2. Add the remaining water, biga, flours, and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon until well combined (about 5 minutes). The dough should not pull away from the side of the bowl.
3. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured board and knead for several minutes to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.
4. Turn the dough into a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise at room temperature until it has almost tripled in volume (3-5 hours). The dough can also be left to rise overnight or in the refrigerator.
5. Let the dough come back to room temperature before shaping. Prepare your work surface and a scraper by floursing them well.
6. Turn the dough out onto the work surface, but do not punch down. Divide into 3 equal pieces.
7. If using fillings (walnuts, pistachios, or olives), flatten each piece of dough slightly and sprinkle with an even amount of filling.
8. Fold in the sides of the dough and roll each piece into a ball, being gentle but firm enough to keep the skin taut.
9. Place the loaves on floured parchment paper set on baking sheets. Cover with a heavy cloth and let rise until doubled (about 1 hour).
10. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Bake the loaves for 15 minutes.
11. Remove the parchment from the loaves and slide them directly onto the baking sheets. Continue baking until crusty and golden, about 30-35 minutes.
